# notes-pdf/

**Derived artifacts — do not edit by hand.** PDF exports of the 16 markdown
study guides in `data/guides/`, generated by `scripts/export_pdfs.py` for
exam-day offline use (copy-paste from prepared material is allowed at the
exam; the app itself may not be runnable there). Regenerate any time with:

```
python scripts/export_pdfs.py
```

The markdown guides in `data/guides/` remain the single source of truth.
If a guide changes, re-run the script (use `--skip-existing` to rebuild only
what is stale, then `--merge-only` is NOT needed — the default run re-merges).

## Properties

- **Real text layer** — fully selectable and searchable (printed by headless
  Chromium, never rasterized). UTF-8 typography (em-dashes, `›` titles,
  quotes) intact.
- **A4**, 16/15/18/15 mm margins, no URL/date headers or footers.
- **Bookmarks:** every per-guide PDF has an outline (guide title + all of its
  H2 sections). `SB5-MAI-complete-notes.pdf` has a two-level outline —
  **214 bookmarks**: 16 guide-level entries + 198 H2-section entries.
- **Order in the combined volume:** `00-overview`, lectures `01–11`
  (numeric), then `90, 91, 92, 93, 94` — same as the ingest/course order.
- Code blocks are monospaced and wrap (never clipped); markdown tables render
  as real bordered tables; H1/H2/H3 hierarchy is visually distinct.

Generated 2026-06-12 (Edge headless + python-markdown + PyMuPDF). Whether
these derived PDFs get committed to git is the user's decision.
